# Platform Intelligence — UGC (user-generated-content style asset)

Practitioner-grade reference for **UGC-style assets** — the creator-authentic, native-looking content
used for paid social + organic that *doesn't read as an ad*. Consumed by `write-ad`,
`brief-shortform`, `produce-video`, `write-social`. The defining property: it looks like a real person
made it on their phone, because the feed punishes anything that smells produced. (The platform-native
craft — retention curves, format specs — lives in the per-platform packs; this is the *style* pack.)

When `last_verified` exceeds 90 days, re-verify the creative norms (they shift fast with platform trends).

---

## 1. UGC Angles (Hook Taxonomy, reframed)

### Angle 1 — Talking-head testimonial ("I tried X for 30 days")

- **Definition:** One person, phone-front-camera, telling a first-person story of using the product;
  unscripted-feeling; specific result.
- **Identifying signal:** Selfie framing, real room, conversational, a concrete claim with a number.
- **Best for:** trust-building, paid social, product proof.

### Angle 2 — Screen-record + voiceover (the "let me show you")

- **Definition:** Phone/desktop screen-record of the product in use with a casual VO; the doing IS the content.
- **Identifying signal:** Real screen, casual narration, no polished motion graphics.
- **Best for:** software/app demos in UGC style, "how I use it" content.

### Angle 3 — Problem/reaction (pattern-interrupt open)

- **Definition:** Opens on a relatable frustration or a surprising reaction, then reveals the product
  as the turn. The hook is the emotion in second one.
- **Identifying signal:** A felt problem or a genuine reaction in frame 1; native, unpolished.
- **Best for:** scroll-stopping paid creative, top-of-funnel.

---

## 2. Format Constraints

| Constraint | Value | reference |
|---|---|---|
| Hook | the first **1 second** must stop the scroll (problem/result/reaction) | retention practice |
| Look | phone-shot, native, *not* studio-lit or agency-graded | creator practice |
| Length | 15–40s for paid social; ≤60s organic; platform pack governs the cap | platform packs |
| Aspect | 9:16 vertical (the UGC default); 1:1 only if the surface needs it | platform packs |
| Captions | burned-in, native-style (auto-caption look), sound-off legible | social practice |
| One person, one use | a single authentic person + one real use case per asset | creator practice |
| Talking-head energy | conversational, direct-address, imperfect > scripted-stiff | creator practice |
| CTA | soft, native ("link in bio", "I'll put it below"), not a hard ad-CTA | creator practice |
| Disclosure | label paid partnerships per platform rules (FTC/#ad) | platform policy (hard) |

---

## 4. Anti-Patterns

| Pattern | Penalty | Detection | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Looks like a produced ad | skipped; the feed punishes ad-gloss | studio lighting, motion graphics, slick grade | creator practice |
| Slow/branded intro | dropped before the hook | logo or setup before the value/emotion | retention practice |
| Scripted-stiff delivery | reads fake; trust dies | obvious teleprompter cadence, no human imperfection | creator practice |
| Multiple claims/uses | diluted; no single takeaway | >1 use case crammed in | creator practice |
| Hard direct-response CTA | breaks the native feel | "BUY NOW" energy in a UGC frame | creator practice |
| Missing #ad/disclosure | policy violation + trust/legal risk | paid content without disclosure | platform policy (hard) |
| Sound-dependent (no captions) | muted viewers get nothing | no burned-in captions | social practice |

---

## 5. Playbook / Tactical Sequence

**Highest-leverage tactic:** one authentic person, phone-shot, hooking a real problem/result in the
first second and showing ONE genuine use — native enough that it doesn't read as an ad.

| # | Step | Concrete action | Success marker |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Pick the angle | testimonial / screen-record / problem-reaction (§1) | matches the surface + offer |
| 2 | Write the hook (1s) | the problem/result/reaction stated/shown in frame 1 | a stranger wouldn't scroll past |
| 3 | Keep it one-use | a single authentic use case, one claim, one person | no firehose |
| 4 | Shoot native | phone, real room/screen, natural light, conversational VO | does NOT look produced |
| 5 | Caption native | burned-in auto-caption style; sound-off legible | readable muted |
| 6 | Soft CTA | native ask ("link below"), not a hard DR push | feels like a recommendation |
| 7 | Disclose | #ad / partnership label per platform if paid | compliant |
| 8 | Test variants + measure | multiple hooks; which held attention → `/measure-results` | next UGC batch compounds |

---

## 7. CTA / Conversion Norms

| Placement | When it works | When it fails | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Soft verbal + on-screen ("link below") | native to UGC; feels like a tip | hard "BUY NOW" overlay | creator practice |
| End, after the genuine use | viewer got value first | front-loaded pitch | creator practice |
| Caption / bio link | platform-native path | relying on a non-clickable in-video link | platform packs |

---

## 8. Open Questions / Known Unknowns

- UGC creative norms shift fast with platform trends; the "doesn't look like an ad" bar moves — re-verify per quarter.
- The authenticity/conversion relationship is creative-dependent; test hooks rather than assuming a template wins.
- Optimal length + aspect are governed by the destination platform pack, not this style pack alone.
